name: Gallium
description: GaAs/GaN wafers, RF chips, power electronics. Byproduct of bauxite and zinc; irreplaceable in defense/RF/power apps.

substitutability.source_note: No effective substitute for GaAs/GaN in many defense apps.

lead_time_years.source_note: Years to stand up primary production; no US base today.

static.notes: USGS estimated a full cessation of Chinese gallium exports could cut US GDP by ~$3.1B. China banned gallium exports to the US in Dec 2024.

name: Indium
description: ITO for displays/touchscreens; some semiconductor use. Byproduct of zinc smelting; supply capped by zinc capacity.

scale.source_note: USGS ~990 t/yr refined; Asia-Pacific ~70% of total.

substitutability.source_note: Moderate — recycling is significant for Korea/Japan.

lead_time_years.source_note: Industry reading: indium is a byproduct of zinc mining, so scaling doesn't require greenfield mine development. Recycling covers a significant share of Korea/Japan demand. Adding new primary refining capacity ~2 years. Paper does not quantify.

static.notes: Capped by zinc production volumes; cannot scale on demand. Reserves spread across Canada, China, US, Peru, Russia.

name: Neodymium
description: NdFeB permanent magnets for cooling fans, drives, storage. Light-REE magnet pair with praseodymium.

scale.source_note: Global REE production 2025; magnet-REEs a subset.

substitutability.source_note: Low-moderate — some emerging alternatives, MP Materials + Lynas + Serra Verde diversifying supply.

lead_time_years.source_note: Paper §1B implies multi-year lead time for non-China REE processing capacity. Industry-informed estimate ~4 years for MP Materials / Lynas / Serra Verde-style refining ramps.

static.notes: China deliberately excluded Nd/Pr from Apr 2025 controls — reserving leverage for harder-to-source elements. Magnet-REE demand projected to triple by 2035.

name: Dysprosium
description: Heavy REE; high-temperature stability additive in NdFeB magnets. THE chokepoint of the magnet-REE set. Raw supply hostage to Myanmar civil conflict.

scale.source_note: Subset of heavy-REE separation; genuinely scarce.

substitutability.source_note: None at scale — paper explicitly identifies as the highest-criticality AI mineral.

lead_time_years.source_note: Long — new heavy-REE separation capacity requires years.

static.notes: Kachin Independence Army seized mining towns Chipwi/Pangwa in Oct 2024. China restricted 7 heavy REEs incl. Dy on Apr 4, 2025; carmakers globally cut/halted production within weeks. Only non-China commercial milestone: Lynas Malaysia.

name: Copper
description: Circuitry, interconnects, heat exchange, and the entire power-delivery layer for data centers. Diversified mining, China-heavy refining.

substitutability.source_note: Low — aluminum substitutes some uses; recycling covers 20–35% of refined supply.

lead_time_years.source_note: Paper §3A (reference table): '~17 years discovery-to-production, some estimates to 25.' Direct paper figure; upgraded from `estimate` to `hard`. Value 17.0 unchanged.

static.notes: NOT a concentration problem per paper — a volume/demand story. Structural deficit potentially >6 Mt/yr by early 2030s. COMEX broke $6/lb for the first time in Jan 2026.
static.modeling_caveat: Per-stage HHI split done and refiner edges now modelled across the paper's named copper countries (China ~47% from paper; Chile, DRC, Peru and US distributing the ex-China share proxied from mining prominence, since the paper does not detail ex-China refining). Refining HHI reads roughly 0.29 as a result. The remaining exposure the model does not represent is the scale axis — the paper's copper story is a ~23 Mt/yr volume against a 17–25 year mine lead time, i.e. a scale/demand constraint, not a concentration one. Concentration is no longer absorbing that risk.

name: High Bandwidth Memory (HBM)
description: Stacked memory sitting next to AI accelerators — co-equal bottleneck with the GPU per the paper. Sold out through 2026 across all three suppliers.

substitutability.source_note: No substitute at scale for HBM in AI accelerators.

lead_time_years.source_note: New memory fabs + qualification. Sold out through 2026.

static.notes: Korea-heavy supplier base (SK Hynix + Samsung) distinct from the Taiwan foundry choke. Micron redirecting from consumer to data center caused a consumer-memory shortage.

name: CoWoS Advanced Packaging
description: TSMC's advanced-packaging process that combines logic + HBM on a silicon interposer. Its capacity is a separate bottleneck from wafer fab and has repeatedly gated GPU output — a leading indicator of GPU supply.

substitutability.source_note: Samsung has an alternative but yields lag significantly.

lead_time_years.source_note: Paper §2E discusses CoWoS as gating GPU output but does not give a specific lead time. Industry-informed estimate ~2.5 years for a new advanced-packaging line to qualify.

static.notes: Distinct from wafer fabrication capacity, but likewise concentrated at TSMC.

name: NdFeB Permanent Magnets
description: The finished magnets in cooling fans, hard drives, and precision servos. Nd for the base magnet, Dy for high-temperature stability.

scale.source_note: Nd-Pr + Dy-Tb magnet pairs projected to grow ~7× to ~$70B by 2040.

substitutability.source_note: Low-moderate — some ferrite alternatives but with performance penalty.

lead_time_years.source_note: Industry reading: new NdFeB magnet factory construction + qualification ~3 years. Paper does not quantify separately from the underlying dysprosium and neodymium lead times.

static.notes: Manufacturing itself is China-concentrated even where mining/refining is diversifying.

name: RF & Power Semiconductors
description: Discrete RF/power components based on GaAs/GaN. The transitive path from gallium into networking, optical, and power-delivery hardware — the actual downstream of gallium in the AI chain, since GPUs are CMOS.

substitutability.source_note: Some silicon-carbide alternatives for power; RF harder to substitute.

lead_time_years.source_note: Industry reading: new RF/power semi fab qualification ~3 years, gated by upstream gallium supply (see gallium lead_time). Paper §1A discusses the chokepoint but does not quantify recovery time separately.

static.notes: Intermediate node — added specifically so gallium's downstream exposure emerges through a real supply path instead of being hardcoded.

name: China
description: Dominant across gallium (~99%), heavy-REE separation (~99%), light-REE mining (~60%), indium refining (~60%), copper refining (~47%). Uses export controls as policy lever.

static.notes: Banned gallium exports to the US Dec 2024; restricted 7 heavy REEs incl. dysprosium Apr 4 2025.

name: Taiwan
description: Home of TSMC. Single point of failure for ~90% of leading-edge (3nm/2nm) capacity. The quantified Taiwan risk.

static.notes: Jan 2026 US–Taiwan deal added TSMC US fabs as a partial hedge.

name: United States
description: Home of most chip designers, all named data centers, most equipment (ex-ASML), MP Materials REE mining, growing merchant power / utility consolidation.

name: Netherlands
description: Home of ASML — the single most irreplaceable link in the chip chain (~90% lithography, sole EUV maker).

name: South Korea
description: Home of SK Hynix and Samsung. The Korea-heavy HBM supplier base — distinct from the Taiwan foundry choke.

name: Japan
description: Home of Tokyo Electron; secondary indium refiner; small gallium producer. Recycling-heavy for indium.

name: Myanmar
description: Upstream feeder into Chinese REE refining. Raw supply hostage to civil conflict — the fragile upstream to dysprosium and neodymium.

name: Kachin State
description: Ionic-clay heavy-REE deposits that feed Chinese refineries. The Kachin Independence Army seized mining towns Chipwi and Pangwa in October 2024 — the paper's canonical 'the news says Kachin, the impact is dysprosium' example.

static.notes: Event feeds will name the towns and the KIA — not 'dysprosium'.

name: Malaysia
description: Site of Lynas's REE separation plant — the only non-China commercial-scale heavy-REE separation milestone.

name: Australia
description: Home of Lynas — one of the few large REE producers outside China.

name: Brazil
description: Emerging light-REE producer via Serra Verde. Small share today; matters for diversification trajectory.

name: Chile
description: #1 copper producer at ~25% of world output.

name: Democratic Republic of the Congo
description: #2 copper producer.

name: Peru
description: #3 copper producer.

name: Canada
description: Secondary gallium refiner; indium refiner; large indium reserves.

name: NVIDIA
description: GPUs; dominant in AI training/inference. CUDA software moat. ~90% of HBM from SK Hynix.

financial_cushion.source_note: Strong FCF and cash pile, but heavy customer + supplier concentration. Placeholder — retune when we have real distributions.

static.notes: Market cap ~$5.0T (June 2026). CUDA lock-in is the real moat.

substitutability.source_note: Paper §2C: no substitute at scale for CUDA + H100/B100 in frontier AI training runs. AMD MI300 is a partial alternative at inference (paper cites qualification lag); custom silicon (TPU, Trainium) is captive and requires 2-3yr redesign. Industry-informed estimate — sub is low but nonzero because MI300 does substitute at inference workloads.

lead_time_years.source_note: Paper §2C-D: NVIDIA's chip development cadence — A100 → H100 was ~2.5yr; H100 → B100 similar. Building a competing training accelerator requires ~3yr redesign + qualification even for AMD or a captive ASIC. Industry-informed estimate.

name: Broadcom
description: Custom AI silicon (ASICs) for hyperscalers + networking. Q3 AI-chip guidance misses now move the whole sector.

financial_cushion.source_note: Large, diversified across networking + ASIC design services.

name: AMD
description: GPUs (Instinct MI-series) + CPUs; second-largest AI-GPU maker, distant #2 to NVIDIA. Cross-exposure to PC/smartphone cycles.

name: TSMC
description: ~90% share at 3nm/2nm; ~60-70% of the overall foundry market. Advanced nodes (≤7nm) ~74% of wafer revenue. Raising sub-5nm prices 5-10% in 2026 — customers have no alternative.

substitutability.source_note: Paper §2B: 'no second source at comparable yield and volume until at least 2027'; TSMC ~90% share at 3nm/2nm. Direct paper quotes; upgraded from `estimate` to `hard`. Value 0.05 unchanged.

lead_time_years.source_note: Paper reference table: 'New leading-edge fab 3–5 years, >$20B (now often $30B+).'

financial_cushion.source_note: Great margins, cash-rich, but capex ~$38B/yr and Taiwan concentration.

static.notes: Agreed to major US investment (~$165-250B) under Jan 2026 US-Taiwan deal.

name: Samsung Electronics
description: Both a foundry (~8-12% share, 3nm yields stuck at 30-40%) and an HBM supplier (~17-22% share, recovering after qualification delays).

substitutability.source_note: Paper: Samsung foundry is an alternative but '3nm yields stuck at 30-40%'; memory unit is one of three HBM suppliers with mutual substitution possible.

lead_time_years.source_note: Paper reference table: 'HBM capacity + qualification: long; sold out through 2026.'

financial_cushion.source_note: Huge diversified conglomerate; foundry unit struggling but memory + consumer businesses cushion.

name: Intel
description: Integrated device manufacturer; foundry ambitions ride on the 18A node (not at industry-standard yield until ~2027). Foundry unit deeply unprofitable.

financial_cushion.source_note: Foundry unit losing money; PC market cross-exposure. Lower cushion despite balance-sheet size.

name: SK Hynix
description: ~57-62% of HBM (2025). NVIDIA's primary supplier (~90% of NVIDIA's HBM).

substitutability.source_note: Paper: three HBM suppliers exist, but SK Hynix is NVIDIA's primary at ~90% single-source dependence — substitution takes qualification cycles.

lead_time_years.source_note: Paper reference table: 'HBM capacity + qualification: long; sold out through 2026.'

financial_cushion.source_note: Tight mutual dependence with NVIDIA — customer concentration risk works both ways.

name: Micron
description: ~21% HBM share; US-based. Redirecting output from consumer memory to AI data centers.

substitutability.source_note: Paper: three HBM suppliers exist with Micron at ~21% share; mutual substitution possible with qualification delays.

lead_time_years.source_note: Paper reference table: 'HBM capacity + qualification: long; sold out through 2026.'

name: ASML
description: EUV lithography monopoly — ~90% of lithography market, sole EUV maker. Backlog ~$45B, sold out into 2027. The single most irreplaceable link in the chain.

substitutability.source_note: Paper §2D: 'the only EUV maker' — no working competitor at any node. This is a direct paper claim about ASML's position, not an inference; upgraded from `estimate` to `hard` in the static axes audit. Value 0.02 unchanged.

lead_time_years.source_note: Paper: 'sold out into 2027' + no alternative EUV supplier exists to be scaled up.

financial_cushion.source_note: Near-monopoly with multi-year backlog. Export-control exposure but demand utterly saturated.

name: Applied Materials
description: Touches more fab steps than anyone — deposition, etch, implant.

substitutability.source_note: Paper: 'touches more fab steps than anyone' — spans deposition/etch/implant with limited direct competitors per step.

lead_time_years.source_note: Paper: 'Entire equipment chain sold out into 2027.'

name: Lam Research
description: Etch and deposition — physically builds the 3D stacks HBM requires. Higher exposure to memory-supercycle than most equipment peers.

substitutability.source_note: Paper: 'physically builds the 3D stacks HBM requires' — specialized etch/deposition capability.

lead_time_years.source_note: Paper: 'Entire equipment chain sold out into 2027.'

name: KLA
description: Inspection and metrology across the fab.

substitutability.source_note: Paper: inspection/metrology role — narrower than deposition/etch, but still a specialized niche with few alternatives at leading-edge.

lead_time_years.source_note: Paper: 'Entire equipment chain sold out into 2027.'

name: Tokyo Electron
description: Coating, etch, cleaning. Japan-based; secondary export-control exposure.

substitutability.source_note: Paper: coating/etch/cleaning tools; overlapping role with Applied Materials + Lam gives some substitution but Japan-heavy production is its own export-control axis.

lead_time_years.source_note: Paper: 'Entire equipment chain sold out into 2027.'

name: Marvell
description: Custom silicon #2 to Broadcom; key co-design partner for hyperscaler ASICs (Amazon Trainium collab).

name: Google (Alphabet)
description: Google TPU custom silicon (designed with Broadcom); Google Cloud hyperscale data centers.

name: Amazon
description: AWS hyperscale data centers; Trainium/Inferentia custom silicon (Marvell co-design).

name: Microsoft
description: Azure hyperscale data centers; Maia custom silicon. $15B UAE investment + restarting Three Mile Island via Constellation PPA.

name: Meta
description: Data centers for own AI + advertising; MTIA custom silicon. $10B Louisiana build chasing power-rich siting.

name: xAI
description: Operator of the Colossus data center in Memphis (2 GW, 555k GPUs, plans for 1M+).

static.modeling_caveat: Inbound HHI reads high because NVIDIA is the only modelled input. Real inputs include power, cooling, land and skilled labour — xAI is less single-sourced than the number implies.

name: OpenAI
description: Operator of the Stargate program (~$100B) — Abilene TX plus MI, WI, WY, NM, GA, OH, PA.

static.modeling_caveat: Inbound HHI reads high because NVIDIA is the only modelled input. Real inputs include power, cooling, land and skilled labour — OpenAI is less single-sourced than the number implies.

name: Constellation Energy
description: Largest US merchant power; ~60 GW including the biggest US nuclear fleet. Restarting Three Mile Island Unit 1 (~2028); bought Calpine (~$16B).

name: Vistra
description: ~45 GW across gas, nuclear, coal, solar, storage.

name: Talen Energy
description: ~13 GW; anchored by Susquehanna nuclear.

name: NextEra Energy
description: Largest US utility; Florida Power & Light + huge renewables/storage. ~21 GW data center pipeline, ~33 GW renewables backlog. Acquiring Dominion (~$67B).

name: Duke Energy
description: Southeast regulated utility; AI demand far above historic pace.

name: Siemens Energy
description: Transformers AND gas turbines, both with multi-year backlogs. The physical chokepoint behind power.

substitutability.source_note: Paper lists three companies in the same role (Siemens Energy, GE Vernova, Hitachi Energy) — mutual substitution possible in principle but all three carry the same multi-year backlog.

lead_time_years.source_note: Paper reference table: 'Grid transformer: ~5-year backlog.'

name: GE Vernova
description: Transformers, gas turbines, wind — multi-year backlogs.

substitutability.source_note: Paper lists three companies in the same role — mutual substitution possible in principle, but all carry the same multi-year backlog.

lead_time_years.source_note: Paper reference table: 'Grid transformer: ~5-year backlog.'

name: Quanta Services
description: Grid construction — the pipeline that connects new generation to new load.

static.modeling_caveat: Inbound HHI reads 1.0 because copper is the only modelled input. Real inputs include steel, transformers, labour and permits — Quanta is not single-sourced to the degree the number suggests.

substitutability.source_note: Paper (grid section): Quanta is one of several grid EPC contractors (Fluor, Bechtel, MYR Group). Contractor substitution is possible but constrained by skilled-labour shortage. Industry-informed estimate — sub reflects real alternatives, not monopoly.

lead_time_years.source_note: Paper (grid section): transmission-project mobilization + build cycle 3-5yr for typical scope, longer for major HV corridors. Industry-informed estimate — lead time is project-cycle, not decade-scale mine/fab.

name: Vertiv
description: Cooling leader; liquid + immersion cooling. Water consumption is a real local-political constraint.

substitutability.source_note: Paper calls Vertiv 'leader' but does not imply monopoly — cooling market has multiple players and liquid/immersion is a fast-growing category with several credible alternatives.

lead_time_years.source_note: Paper: 'electrical equipment lead times 2+ years' as calibration; cooling is less specialized than lithography or transformers.

name: MP Materials
description: Owner-operator of Mountain Pass — the only US light-REE mine at scale, including neodymium.

financial_cushion.source_note: Strategic small US player; single-asset exposure.

name: Lynas Rare Earths
description: Australian REE producer; operates the Malaysia separation plant — the only non-China commercial-scale heavy-REE separation milestone.

name: Mountain Pass Mine
description: The only rare-earth mine at scale in the United States. Light-REE focus including neodymium.

name: Serra Verde
description: Brazilian light-REE mine — emerging supply diversification.

name: Lynas Malaysia Plant
description: The only non-China commercial-scale heavy-REE separation site.

name: Colossus
description: xAI's Memphis, TN data center — 2 GW, 555,000 GPUs today, plans for 1M+; ~$18B invested.

name: Stargate (Abilene)
description: OpenAI's flagship Stargate site — anchor of a ~$100B multi-state program (also MI, WI, WY, NM, GA, OH, PA).

name: Vantage Frontier
description: Texas mega-campus, ~$25B+.

name: The Citadel
description: Switch's Nevada campus — 650 MW, renewable-powered.

name: Three Mile Island Unit 1
description: Nuclear reactor being restarted (~2028) by Constellation to serve a Microsoft PPA. The canonical AI-driven nuclear restart.

name: Nikon (Precision Equipment)
description: Photolithography systems — the #2 lithography vendor globally, but a distant second to ASML. Nikon has zero EUV; its role is DUV ArF/i-line systems at trailing-edge and specialty nodes.

substitutability.source_note: Paper is silent on Nikon specifically. Industry reading: Nikon and Canon can substitute for one another at trailing nodes; neither can substitute for ASML at leading-edge.

lead_time_years.source_note: Industry-informed estimate — shorter than ASML's EUV queue (~3 yrs) but non-trivial for advanced DUV systems.

name: Canon (Semiconductor Equipment)
description: Photolithography systems, primarily i-line and KrF/ArF steppers for mature nodes. Also nanoimprint lithography (NIL) as a long-shot EUV alternative. Distant third behind ASML and Nikon.

substitutability.source_note: Paper is silent on Canon. Industry reading: Canon plays a role at mature nodes only; its NIL platform is not a meaningful EUV substitute in scale terms today.

lead_time_years.source_note: Industry-informed estimate.

name: Hitachi High-Tech
description: Etch and metrology tools; a minor but non-zero player in both categories. Serves memory and mature-node customers primarily.

substitutability.source_note: Paper is silent on Hitachi. Industry reading: minor third-tier player in both etch (vs Lam / TEL) and inspection / metrology (vs KLA / AMAT).

lead_time_years.source_note: Industry-informed estimate.
